guidanceNote: This may be given earlier with provider approval; consult provider if wanting order to administer early

internalNotes: Standard monthly tiers — same threshold structure as Brixadi monthly

idealSteps: The injection is not yet overdue (less than 3 weeks since last administration). Please consult the early administration guidance.
Administer the next injection, regardless of the level of unregulated opioid use.
If the patient reports active use of unregulated opioids, discuss a switch to Sublocade 300 mg and consult prescriber if the patient is interested.*
Conduct a fentanyl dependence assessment before administering:

Administer next injection if the client has **“minimal/no fentanyl dependence”, see definition below**, or if the client has taken at least 8 mg of sublingual buprenorphine in the last 24 hours or received a Brixadi 96 mg (or more) or Sublocade 100 mg (or more) booster in the last 5 weeks.
Administer if they have “moderate fentanyl dependence” and are ok with the risk of some bup-induced withdrawal.
If the patient reports active use of unregulated opioids, discuss a switch to Sublocade 300 mg and consult prescriber if the patient is interested.*
providerNotifications: Consult prescriber if patient does not meet minimal/no, or moderate fentanyl dependence criteria.

idealSteps: Conduct a fentanyl dependence assessment before administering:

Administer next injection if the client has **“minimal/no fentanyl dependence”, see definition below**, or if the client has taken at least 8 mg of sublingual buprenorphine in the last 24 hours or received a Brixadi 96 mg (or more) or Sublocade 100 mg (or more) booster in the last 5 weeks.
If the patient reports active use of unregulated opioids, discuss a switch to Sublocade 300 mg and consult prescriber if the patient is interested.*
providerNotifications: Consult prescriber if patient does not meet minimal/no fentanyl dependence criteria.

idealSteps: **Consult a prescriber in real-time** before administering. The goal is to avoid having the patient leave without guidance. You may consult the ordering prescriber, ORCA Center provider, ORCA Senior Medical Lead or DESC medical director.

internalNotes: 300 mg with 2 or less prior doses: longer window to administer regardless, then conditional

internalNotes: 300 mg well-established: widest administer-regardless window before conditional tiers

idealSteps: **Consult a prescriber in real-time** before administering. The goal is to avoid having the patient leave without guidance. You may consult the ordering provider, ORCA Center provider, ORCA Senior Medical Lead or DESC medical director.
A booster dose may be administered any time if these requirements are met:
The client wants the booster dose
At least 24 hours has passed since the last LAI-buprenorphine injection
No more than 4 weeks has passed since last LAI-buprenorphine injection (else, administer the regular maintenance dose)
The client continues to report use of unregulated fentanyl or other unregulated opioids during the present day
providerNotifications: If the client is reporting NO use of unregulated opioids, please consult a provider. The main risk in this situation is too much buprenorphine causing sedation.

fields.label: Date of last regular maintenance dose of Sublocade

options.label: Sublocade 300 mg — 1 or 2 prior injections without gaps >6 weeks
